heading: Effect of removal by the President or failure of consent of the Senate
label: 3749 Effect of removal by the President or failure of consent of the Senate

Delegation of FunctionsFor assignment of functions of President under subsec. (a) of this section, see section 2(d) of Ex. Ord. No. 13358, , 69 F.R. 58797, set out as a note under , The President.Sept. 28, 2004section 301 of Title 3
amendments: Amendments2018—, renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–282, § 118(b)section 738 of this titleSubsecs. (a), (b). , substituted “section 3740” for “section 729”.Pub. L. 115–282, § 123(b)(2)

children.label: a
children.text: The President may, for cause, remove the name of any officer from a list of selectees established under .section 3740 of this title

children.label: b
children.text: If the Senate, where required, does not consent to the appointment of an officer whose name is on a list of selectees established under , that officer’s name shall be removed from the list.section 3740 of this title

children.label: c
children.text: An officer whose name is removed from a list of selectees under subsection (a) or (b) continues to be eligible for consideration for promotion. If selected for promotion by the next selection board and promoted, that officer shall be assigned the date of rank and precedence that would have been assigned if the officer’s name had not been previously removed. However, if the officer is not selected by the next selection board, or if the officer’s name is again removed from the list of selectees, the officer shall be considered for all purposes as having twice failed of selection for promotion.
